# Consul 2.0.x

We are pleased to announce the following Consul updates.

## Version number update

To align with IBM's software versioning patterns, this Consul release increments the version numbering to v2.0.0. Moving forward, version numbers increment annually. Feature releases occur twice a year, with v2.1 scheduled for release in October 2026.

Consul v2.0.0 has extended Enterprise support options for longer contract periods. Previous Consul Enterprise releases will be maintained according to existing [Long Term Support contracts](/consul/docs/enterprise/lts).

## Release highlights

- **Multi-port routing for service mesh**: Consul now supports service mesh traffic for services with multiple ports. To route multi-port services, you must configure a sidecar proxy's `upstream` to recognize the port names and numbers. When transparent proxy mode is not enabled, applications can use `localhost:<bind-port>` to send secure service mesh traffic to an upstream service. When transparent proxy mode is enabled, multi-port services use the Consul DNS syntax `<port-name>.<sni>.virtual.consul`.
- **Global rate limiting for Consul RPC**:
- **CyberArk Workload Identity Manager (Venafi Firefly) support**:
- **Increased API Gateway scaling limit**:
- **New resource types for OpenShift clusters**:
- **Opt-in telemetry reporting**: Consul now includes an opt-in telemetry reporting feature that is disabled by default. After you opt-in to telemetry reporting, we collect anonymized data about your cluster's operations and aggregate it for analysis. This data helps us understand how you use Consul so that we can improve the software with each subsequent release. For a complete list of telemetry data we collect, refer to [Consul agent telemetry reporting reference](/consul/docs/reference/agent/telemetry/reporting). To enable reporting on your cluster, refer to [Opt-in to telemetry data reporting](/consul/docs/monitor/telemetry#opt-in-to-data-reporting).
- **Expired certificate discovery**: This release introduces the [`/agent/metrics/certificates` HTTP API endpoint](consul/api-docs/agent/metrics), which returns detailed information about certificate expiration status across the Consul infrastructure, including CA roots, intermediates, leaf certificates, and agent TLS certificates. Certificate expiration telemetry is configurable in the [Consul agent's `telemetry` block](/consul/docs/reference/agent/configuration-file/telemetry#certificate-expiration) and exportable to Prometheus. For instructions on monitoring certificate expiration, including Promtheus alerts you can enable, refer to [Monitor certificate expiration](/consul/docs/monitor/telemetry/certificate-expiration).

## Upgrading

For more detailed information, please refer to the [upgrade details page](/consul/docs/upgrade/version-specific) and the changelogs.

## Known issues

There are no known issues at this time. These release notes are updated as issues are discovered.

[Report issues on Github](https://github.com/hashicorp/consul/issues).
